Class Sets

java.lang.Object
org.eclipse.collections.impl.factory.Sets
public final class Sets extends Object
Set algebra operations are available in this class as static utility.

Most operations are non-destructive, i.e. no input sets are modified during execution. The exception is operations ending in "Into." These accept the target collection of the final calculation as the first parameter.

Some effort is made to return a SortedSet if any input set is sorted, but this is not guaranteed (e.g., this will not be the case for collections proxied by Hibernate). When in doubt, specify the target collection explicitly with the "Into" version. This class should be used to create instances of MutableSet, ImmutableSet and FixedSizeSet

Mutable Examples: 

MutableSet<String> emptySet = Sets.mutable.empty();
MutableSet<String> setWith = Sets.mutable.with("a", "b", "c");
MutableSet<String> setOf = Sets.mutable.of("a", "b", "c");
Immutable Examples: 
ImmutableSet<String> emptySet = Sets.immutable.empty();
ImmutableSet<String> setWith = Sets.immutable.with("a", "b", "c");
ImmutableSet<String> setOf = Sets.immutable.of("a", "b", "c");
FixedSize Examples: 
FixedSizeSet<String> emptySet = Sets.fixedSize.empty();
FixedSizeSet<String> setWith = Sets.fixedSize.with("a", "b", "c");
FixedSizeSet<String> setOf = Sets.fixedSize.of("a", "b", "c");

  • Field Details

    • immutable

      public static final ImmutableSetFactory immutable

  • Method Details

    • adapt

      public static <T> MutableSet<T> adapt(Set<T> set)
      Adapts the given Set to the MutableSet interface.

      The returned MutableSet is backed by the provided set, meaning changes made to the returned set will affect the original set and vice versa.

      This method does not create a copy of the provided set, making it a lightweight way to use the Eclipse Collections API with existing JDK sets.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the element type
      Parameters:
      set - the set to adapt
      Returns:
      a MutableSet view of the original set
      Since:
      9.0.

    • union

      public static <E> MutableSet<E> union(Set<? extends E> setA, Set<? extends E> setB)
      Returns the union of two given sets in a new MutableSet.

      Union is the addition of distinct elements of two sets. Common elements are taken only once. No input set is modified.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      Parameters:
      setA - the first set
      setB - the second set
      Returns:
      a new MutableSet which contains the union of setA and setB
      See Also:
      • invalid reference 
        #unionInto(MutableSet, Set, Set)
      • invalid reference 
        #newSet(Set, Set)

    • unionInto

      public static <E, R extends Set<E>> R unionInto(R targetSet, Set<? extends E> setA, Set<? extends E> setB)
      Performs the union of two given sets into the supplied targetSet.

      Neither input set is modified. The returned set is the same instance as targetSet.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      R - the mutable set type
      Parameters:
      targetSet - the set that will receive the union
      setA - the first set
      setB - the second set
      Returns:
      the targetSet containing the union of setA and setB
      See Also:
      • fillSet(Set, Procedure2, Set...)

    • unionAll

      public static <E> MutableSet<E> unionAll(Set<? extends E>... sets)
      Returns the union of all given sets in a new MutableSet.

      No input set is modified.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      Parameters:
      sets - the sets to union
      Returns:
      a new MutableSet containing distinct elements of given sets
      See Also:
      • invalid reference 
        #newSet(Set, Set)
      • unionAllInto(Set, Set...)

    • unionAllInto

      public static <E, R extends Set<E>> R unionAllInto(R targetSet, Set<? extends E>... sets)
      Performs the union of all given sets into the supplied targetSet.

      The input sets are not modified. The returned set is the same instance as the targetSet.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      R - the mutable set type
      Parameters:
      targetSet - the set that will receive the union
      sets - the sets to union
      Returns:
      the targetSet containing distinct elements of all given sets
      See Also:
      • fillSet(Set, Procedure2, Set...)

    • intersect

      public static <E> MutableSet<E> intersect(Set<? extends E> setA, Set<? extends E> setB)
      Returns the intersection of two given sets in a new MutableSet.

      Intersection contains elements common to both sets. No input set is modified.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      Parameters:
      setA - the first set
      setB - the second set
      Returns:
      a new MutableSet which contains the intersection of setA and setB
      See Also:
      • invalid reference 
        #intersectInto(MutableSet, Set, Set)
      • invalid reference 
        #newSet(Set, Set)

    • intersectInto

      public static <E, R extends Set<E>> R intersectInto(R targetSet, Set<? extends E> setA, Set<? extends E> setB)
      Performs the intersection of two given sets into the supplied targetSet.

      Neither input set is modified. The returned set is the same instance as targetSet.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      R - the mutable set type
      Parameters:
      targetSet - the set that will receive the intersection
      setA - the first set
      setB - the second set
      Returns:
      the targetSet containing the intersection of setA and setB
      See Also:
      • fillSet(Set, Procedure2, Set...)

    • intersectAll

      public static <E> MutableSet<E> intersectAll(Set<? extends E>... sets)
      Returns the intersection of all given sets in a new MutableSet.

      No input set is modified.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      Parameters:
      sets - the sets to intersect
      Returns:
      a new MutableSet containing the common elements of given sets
      See Also:
      • invalid reference 
        #newSet(Set, Set)
      • intersectAllInto(Set, Set...)

    • intersectAllInto

      public static <E, R extends Set<E>> R intersectAllInto(R targetSet, Set<? extends E>... sets)
      Performs the intersection of all given sets into the supplied targetSet.

      The input sets are not modified. The returned set is the same instance as the targetSet.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      R - the mutable set type
      Parameters:
      targetSet - the set that will receive the intersection
      sets - the sets to intersect
      Returns:
      the targetSet containing common elements of all given sets
      See Also:
      • fillSet(Set, Procedure2, Set...)

    • difference

      public static <E> MutableSet<E> difference(Set<? extends E> minuendSet, Set<? extends E> subtrahendSet)
      Returns the difference of two given sets in a new MutableSet.

      Difference contains elements which are only present in the minuend set, but not in subtrahend set. No input set is modified.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      Parameters:
      minuendSet - the set to subtract from
      subtrahendSet - the set being subtracted
      Returns:
      a new MutableSet which contains the difference of two given sets
      See Also:
      • invalid reference 
        #differenceInto(MutableSet, Set, Set)
      • invalid reference 
        #newSet(Set, Set)

    • differenceInto

      public static <E, R extends Set<E>> R differenceInto(R targetSet, Set<? extends E> minuendSet, Set<? extends E> subtrahendSet)
      Performs the difference of two given sets into the supplied targetSet.

      Neither input set is modified. The returned set is the same instance as the targetSet.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      R - the mutable set type
      Parameters:
      targetSet - the set that will receive the difference
      minuendSet - the set to subtract from
      subtrahendSet - the set being subtracted
      Returns:
      the targetSet containing the difference of minuendSet and subtrahendSet
      See Also:
      • fillSet(Set, Procedure2, Set...)

    • differenceAll

      public static <E> MutableSet<E> differenceAll(Set<? extends E>... sets)
      Returns the difference of first set with respect to all other given sets in a new MutableSet.

      No input set is modified.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      Parameters:
      sets - the sets to difference
      Returns:
      a new MutableSet containing the distinct elements of the first set with respect to the remaining sets
      See Also:
      • invalid reference 
        #newSet(Set, Set)
      • differenceAllInto(Set, Set...)

    • differenceAllInto

      public static <E, R extends Set<E>> R differenceAllInto(R targetSet, Set<? extends E>... sets)
      Performs the difference of first set with respect to all other given sets into the supplied targetSet.

      The input sets are not modified. The returned set is the same instance as the targetSet.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      R - the mutable set type
      Parameters:
      targetSet - the set that will receive the difference
      sets - the sets to difference
      Returns:
      the targetSet containing the distinct elements of the firest set with respect to the remaining sets
      See Also:
      • fillSet(Set, Procedure2, Set...)

    • symmetricDifference

      public static <E> MutableSet<E> symmetricDifference(Set<? extends E> setA, Set<? extends E> setB)
      Returns the symmetric difference of two given sets in a new MutableSet.

      Symmetric difference contains elements which are uncommon to both sets. No input set is modified.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      Parameters:
      setA - the first set
      setB - the second set
      Returns:
      a new MutableSet containing the symmetric difference of the two given sets
      See Also:
      • invalid reference 
        #symmetricDifferenceInto(MutableSet, Set, Set)
      • invalid reference 
        #newSet(Set, Set)

    • symmetricDifferenceInto

      public static <E, R extends Set<E>> R symmetricDifferenceInto(R targetSet, Set<? extends E> setA, Set<? extends E> setB)
      Performs the symmetric difference of two given sets into the supplied targetSet.

      Neither input set is modified. The returned set is the same instance as the targetSet.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      R - the mutable set type
      Parameters:
      targetSet - the set that will receive the symmetric difference
      setA - the first set
      setB - the second set
      Returns:
      the targetSet containing the symmetric difference of setA and setB
      See Also:
      • invalid reference 
        #unionInto(MutableSet, Set, Set)
      • invalid reference 
        #differenceInto(MutableSet, Set, Set)
      • invalid reference 
        #newSet(Set, Set)

    • isSubsetOf

      public static <E> boolean isSubsetOf(Set<? extends E> candidateSubset, Set<? extends E> candidateSuperset)
      Determines if the candidate set is the subset of the supplied set.

      A set is a subset of another if all its elements are contained in the other set.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      Parameters:
      candidateSubset - the potential subset
      candidateSuperset - the potential superset
      Returns:
      true if candidateSubset is a subset of candidateSuperset, false if otherwise

    • isProperSubsetOf

      public static <E> boolean isProperSubsetOf(Set<? extends E> candidateSubset, Set<? extends E> candidateSuperset)
      Determines if the given set is the proper subset of the provided set.

      A proper subset must be smaller than the superset, and all the elements of the subset must be contained in the superset.

      Type Parameters:
      E - the element type
      Parameters:
      candidateSubset - the potential proper subset
      candidateSuperset - the potential proper superset
      Returns:
      true if candidateSubset is a proper subset of candidateSuperset, false if otherwise

    • powerSet

      public static <T> MutableSet<MutableSet<T>> powerSet(Set<T> set)
      Returns the power set of a given set.

      Power set contains all possible subsets of a given set.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the element type
      Parameters:
      set - the set whose power set is to be computed
      Returns:
      the power set of the supplied set

    • cartesianProduct

      public static <A,B> LazyIterable<Pair<A,B>> cartesianProduct(Set<A> set1, Set<B> set2)
      Returns the cartesian product of two sets as a LazyIterable of Pairs.
      Type Parameters:
      A - the element type of set1
      B - the element type of set2
      Parameters:
      set1 - the first set
      set2 - the second set
      Returns:
      the cartesian product of the two given sets
      See Also:
      • cartesianProduct(Set, Set, Function2)

    • cartesianProduct

      public static <A,B,C> LazyIterable<C> cartesianProduct(Set<A> set1, Set<B> set2, Function2<? super A, ? super B, ? extends C> function)
      Returns the cartesian product of two sets, applying the given function to each pair.

      The result is returned as a LazyIterable, which saves memory and improves performance.

      Type Parameters:
      A - element type of set1
      B - element type of set2
      C - the result type of the function
      Parameters:
      set1 - the first set
      set2 - the second set
      function - the function to apply
      Returns:
      the LazyIterable after the function is applied to each pair of the sequence
